name: Herman Wheat'n'honey Starter
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Honey; or brown sugar 1/4 tsp
recipeInstructions:
1. Stir well and keep in the refrigerator.
2. Sprinkle ginger and 1 tablespoon honey or brown sugar over warm water.
3. Sprinkle yeast over this mixture and stir.
4. Let stand in a warm place to double in size, about 10 minutes.
5. Mix milk, remaining honey or brown sugar, flours, and yeast mixture in a glass container (about the size of a gallon Pyrex jar).
6. Stir using only a wooden spoon, as metal objects will retard Herman's growth.
7. Leave the cover loosely on or place a glass plate over the top to allow Herman to breathe.
8. Herman may double or even triple during vigorous rising.
9. Place Herman in a warm place overnight.
10. Next day, refrigerate loosely covered and stir each day. This is important as more gases form and are released during stirring, which may cause the starter to appear darker on top before stirring each day, but this is okay.
11. On the 5th day, measure out 1 cup Herman for baking and another cup to give to a friend (if desired), then feed the remaining Herman using the same method described above.
12. From day 5 onward, you may bake with Herman any day you wish, ensuring you have at least one full cup left for feeding.
13. Feed Herman every five days. If you have a lot left, use only 1 tablespoon honey or brown sugar. If you have 1 cup or slightly more left, add the same ingredients as on the 5th day.
14. When sharing a cup of Herman with friends, they should feed Herman using the same method.
